operator_directive.summary: Step away from .md operator-facing files; everything deterministic and machine-ingestable from now on; .md only on operator request or for operator-created prose/research.
operator_directive.exact_quote: do not use .md files when possible. i am trying to step away from operator facing files, only machine readable from now on. whatever file, codex or prtocol tells you to create a .md file should be changed. markdown is for prose, research. but everything should be determiministic and machine ingestable/readable from now on. markdown is only created on operator request

in_scope_surfaces.current_behavior: Walks .md files in .GOV/task_packets/stubs/ as the source of truth and generates .contract.json projections from them.
in_scope_surfaces.desired_behavior: Walk .contract.json files as the source of truth; treat .md as an optional legacy projection that, when present, must remain in sync. Drop .md generation by default.
in_scope_surfaces.notes: Until this script is inverted, .json-only stubs are invisible to the discovery surfaces below. Stubs still exist as durable record on disk; only discovery is impaired.

in_scope_surfaces.current_behavior: Markdown table rows mapping Base WP IDs to active packet/stub paths (currently always .md-shaped).
in_scope_surfaces.desired_behavior: JSON record (e.g., .GOV/roles_shared/records/wp_traceability_registry.json) with one entry per Base WP ID, including state token, active path, supersession history. Markdown projection (if any) generated on demand and never edited by hand.

in_scope_surfaces.current_behavior: Markdown roll-up of WP status across Ready for Dev, In Progress, Done, Superseded, Backlog Stubs sections; updated by hand and gated by task-board-check.
in_scope_surfaces.desired_behavior: JSON record (e.g., .GOV/roles_shared/records/task_board.json) holding the same status tokens with stable entry IDs. Markdown projection generated on demand only.
in_scope_surfaces.consideration: task-board-check currently enforces .md row format; will need parallel JSON-aware check during transition.

in_scope_surfaces.current_behavior: Autogenerated .md with an AUTOGENERATED block; derived from WP_TRACEABILITY_REGISTRY.md + per-stub .md metadata via build-order-sync.mjs.
in_scope_surfaces.desired_behavior: Build-order produces a JSON snapshot as authority (e.g., .GOV/roles_shared/records/build_order.json); .md projection optional and generated only on demand.

in_scope_surfaces.current_behavior: Various clauses reference .md packet/refinement/microtask/stub artifacts as authority bridges (CX-908, CX-914 already point toward machine-readable but legacy clauses still reference .md authority paths).
in_scope_surfaces.desired_behavior: Codex amendments that reflect the operator's no-.md directive: new model-created governance artifacts default to .json-authority + optional .md projection on operator request; .md is created only for prose/research surfaces.

in_scope_surfaces.current_behavior: Role protocols are .md-authored. They reference .md artifacts as outputs (packet.md, refinement.md, MT-*.md, validation report .md, etc.).
in_scope_surfaces.desired_behavior: Convert protocol bodies to JSON contracts (rule list + handoff schema). Role-facing summary remains as a generated capsule, not authored prose. Reference outputs as .json contracts with optional .md projection.

in_scope_surfaces.current_behavior: Markdown projections coexist with JSON contracts; some tooling still treats .md as authority bridge.
in_scope_surfaces.desired_behavior: Stop generating .md projections by default for new WPs; .json is the authority. Existing .md projections become frozen legacy migration evidence per [CX-914].

out_of_scope_explicit_keep_as_md: README.md files at repo navigation level
Operator-created notes, research, and audits per [GLOBAL-GOVARTIFACTS-019]

blocked_work.impact: Stub exists on disk as .contract.json but is invisible to WP_TRACEABILITY_REGISTRY / TASK_BOARD / BUILD_ORDER discovery until the .md→.json refactor lands.

proposed_phasing.summary: Invert task-packet-stub-contracts.mjs to walk .contract.json files as source of truth; treat .md as optional legacy projection.
scope: writeAllStubContracts becomes a no-op when .contract.json is hand-authored; only regenerate when an .md source exists (legacy mode).
Add a --check-md-projection flag for the legacy bridge mode.
Update readStubContractForMarkdownPath callers (build-order-sync.mjs:170 + :566) to accept either .md or .contract.json paths.
implementation_notes: Added listStubContractFiles() that walks .contract.json files in stubs/.

writeAllStubContracts() now walks .contract.json files; if .md sibling exists, regenerates contract from .md (legacy mode); if no .md, leaves the hand-authored contract alone. Backstop picks up legacy .md without an existing contract.

checkAllStubContracts() now walks .contract.json files; if .md sibling exists, derives expected from .md and requires exact match (legacy drift check); if no .md, validates the contract against validateHandAuthoredStubContract schema. Backstop catches legacy .md with no contract.
Added validateHandAuthoredStubContract() pure function that asserts the minimum schema fields downstream tools need.
16 unit tests in .GOV/roles_shared/tests/task-packet-stub-contracts.test.mjs cover validation rules + path helpers + .md/.json path-agnostic read.
Live check: 196 stub contracts (193 legacy .md-sourced + 3 new .json-only KERNEL-006/007/008) — all OK.

task-packet-stub-contract-check still passes in packet-truth-bundle; the 2 remaining packet-truth-bundle sub-check failures are unrelated pre-existing issues (Atelier-Lens-v2 + Photo-Studio-v2 SUPERSEDED status drift, stale FLAT_PACKET_LEGACY_INVENTORY).

proposed_phasing.summary: Migrate WP_TRACEABILITY_REGISTRY to .json authority with auto-generated .md projection.
scope: Created .GOV/roles_shared/records/wp_traceability_registry.json with the same per-row data, plus a previously-missed third table (Superseded Activation History) discovered during migration parsing.

Strategy switched mid-flight: JSON is authority; .md is now a generated projection regenerated from .json on every write. Readers (10 consumers) continue parsing the .md unchanged — no consumer migration was needed.
Migration is lossless: 161 active entries + 51 superseded activation history rows + 2 historical lineage rows = 214 rows preserved (verified byte-count match with pre-migration .md row count).
Single writer (wp-traceability-set.mjs) refactored to write JSON + regenerate .md via persistRegistry; resolves stub paths for .md, .contract.json, or .json packet paths.

New drift check (wp-traceability-registry-projection-check.mjs) wired into packet-truth-bundle-check — asserts .md matches renderRegistryMarkdown(load(.json)) and fails the bundle if a hand-edit or external write divergence occurs.
implementation_notes: Designed json schema: schema_id, schema_version, authority_surface=MACHINE_CONTRACT, rendered_md_projection_policy=GENERATED_FROM_JSON_DO_NOT_HAND_EDIT, entries[], superseded_activation_history[], historical_lineage[].

Lib at .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/lib/traceability-registry-lib.mjs: loadRegistryJson, writeRegistryJson, parseLegacyMarkdown, renderRegistryMarkdown, persistRegistry, findEntry, upsertEntry, migrateRegistryFromMarkdownIfNeeded.

10 unit tests in .GOV/roles_shared/tests/traceability-registry-lib.test.mjs cover: empty contract shape, JSON round-trip, schema_id rejection, upsert insert/update/preserve, find missing, three-table parser, no-bleed across section boundaries, persist+render round-trip.
Migration runs once via migrateRegistryFromMarkdownIfNeeded; subsequent writers go through upsertEntry + persistRegistry.
Added KERNEL-006/007/008 entries via the writer pointing at .contract.json paths.
just build-order-sync regenerated BUILD_ORDER.md with the new entries visible at priorities 185/180/60.

task-packet-stub-contract-check + new wp-traceability-registry-projection-check both PASS inside packet-truth-bundle. Same 2 pre-existing sub-check failures remain (packet-truth-check Atelier/Photo-Studio supersession drift, stale flat-packet inventory) — unrelated to this refactor.
Readers were intentionally NOT migrated; the auto-generated .md keeps them working without churn. Future phases can migrate readers incrementally without breaking the system.

proposed_phasing.summary: Migrate TASK_BOARD to .json authority with optional .md projection.
scope: Create .GOV/roles_shared/records/task_board.json with the same status tokens.
Migrate the existing .md by one-time conversion + freeze as legacy.

proposed_phasing.summary: Migrate BUILD_ORDER snapshot to .json authority with optional .md projection.
scope: build-order-sync.mjs writes a build_order.json as authority.
.md projection (if any) is generated from the .json on demand.
Drop the AUTOGENERATED block in .md; reference the .json.

proposed_phasing.summary: Codex amendments + role protocol JSON conversion.
scope: Add a Codex clause that makes .contract.json the authority surface for new model-created stubs/packets/refinements/microtasks, with .md only on explicit operator/report-contract request.
Convert role protocol bodies (validator, orchestrator, kernel_builder, etc.) to .json contracts with rule lists + handoff schemas; generated role-facing capsules replace authored prose.
Mark all existing protocol .md as legacy migration evidence per [CX-914].

risks.description: Tooling chain has multiple consumers of .md authority paths. Partial refactor leaves inconsistent surfaces. Mitigation: phase sequencing above is dependency-ordered; gov-check should fail any partial-migration state.
Frozen .md files become misleading legacy authority. Mitigation: add a frontmatter marker LEGACY_FROZEN with pointer to the .json authority + a check that prevents edits.

Operator-facing read of TASK_BOARD/BUILD_ORDER becomes harder if no .md projection is regenerated. Mitigation: keep a generated .md projection available on `just board-view` / `just build-order-view` recipes without making it authority.
Conversion churn during Week 6 stub activation. Mitigation: do Phase 1 immediately so KERNEL-006/007/008 become visible without waiting for the full refactor.

next_actions.description: Operator approves the phasing and Phase 1 (stub-script inversion) starts.
Kernel Builder or governance-scoped session implements MD-ELIM-PHASE-1.
Add WP-KERNEL-006/007/008 rows to WP_TRACEABILITY_REGISTRY (done via PHASE-2 writer, .contract.json paths). KERNEL-006/007/008 now visible in BUILD_ORDER snapshot.
MD-ELIM-PHASE-2 (migrate WP_TRACEABILITY_REGISTRY to .json-primary) - approved and completed.

Operator decision: proceed with MD-ELIM-PHASE-3 (migrate TASK_BOARD.md to .json-primary)? Pattern is the same as PHASE-2: JSON authority + auto-generated .md projection + drift check + single-writer refactor. task-board-set.mjs is the single writer (already identified).
